observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
revise the SCNAE and provide line item for the direct adjustment to equity account totaling ₱4,990,157.30;
provide disclosures in the NFS regarding the restatement for CY  2022 financial statements in compliance with paragraph 57 of IPSAS 3; and
provide schedules of prior period adjustments made which affected the changes of account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-Designate to:
prioritize the finalization of the complete listing of all insurable assets, interest and properties following the guidelines and format prescribed under COA Circular 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018; and
prepare the PIF and submit the same to the GSIS as the basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage of all insurable assets to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by unforeseen circumstances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman, to facilitate the immediate submission of the identified lacking documents for audit purposes to ascertain the legality, validity, and propriety of the paid transactions in adherence with the existing laws, rules, and regulations, and to prevent the issuance of a Notice of Suspensions; and
Municipal Accountant to exercise utmost prudence and diligence in her duties and responsibilities in ensuring that all pertinent documents as discussed above are attached in the disbursement vouchers in adherence to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 prior to the processing of payment for all infrastructure projects and to substantiate the legality, validity, and propriety of the said transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, direct the Project Engineers to:
strictly require all contractors to ensure their entire workforce is wearing the required safety gear or personal protective equipment at all times within the construction project site for the protection and welfare of both the construction workers and the general public;
closely monitor the proper implementation of the CSHP and Standards by the contractors at the project sites by maintaining detailed records of contractor’s non-compliance per site visitation, which can be referenced during future payment processes; and
formulate an internal policy that clearly defines the appropriate sanctions or penalties to be applied to contractors found in violation of the CSHP to ensure accountability and adherence to safety protocols.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to compute claims for hazard pay in accordance with the provisions in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2016. Further, we recommend refunding the overpayment amounting to P37,305.58 for the months of January to November 2023.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
inform the concerned participants and require the immediate refund of the excess payment made for their registration fees in the total amount of ₱150,600.00; and
observe the limit of ₱2,000.00 per day for each participant set forth under NBC No. 563 for subsequent transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO to install a sound internal control, such as specific guidelines on the grant of AICS and other financial assistance to its constituents, to avoid granting assistance in varying amounts to ensure equitable distribution of financial assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly and year-end financial statements and reports within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 347 of RA No. 7160, Section 70 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006; and
The municipal accountant, in coordination with the municipal treasurer, must submit the remaining 11 DVs totaling ₱384,453.31 and the 16 cancelled checks. Henceforth, submit all vouchers within 10 days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provisions of Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
MPDC and Acting Municipal Engineer, in coordination with other implementing offices, to thoroughly review the fund utilization report and Annual Investment Program, as well as, identify specific PPAs related on the share of excise taxes on locally manufactured Virginia-type cigarettes (RA No. 7171) and Burley and Native tobacco (RA No. 8240).
BAC, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Engineer, to include in the CY 2024 APP the procurement of the unimplemented PPAs and ensure and monitor the full implementation of the PPAs programmed under the RA 7171 fund in order to address the needs of the constituents; and
MPDC, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Engineer and Municipal Agriculturist to:
prioritize the immediate identification of projects in order to facilitate the implementation thereof; and
provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each activity in the AIP.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management through the BAC and TWG conduct strict, extensive and efficient bid-evaluation and post-qualification by scrupulously scrutinizing the documents submitted by the bidder particularly the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts, List of Contractor’s Personnel of contractors and List of Contractor’s Equipment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to conduct judicious, rigorous and prompt monitoring and validation of their ongoing and completed IPs. If upon monitoring and validation on the projected or contracted completion date the Management reasonably determined that the completion of the IP will already incur delay, require the Contractor to submit a SWA as of the projected or contracted completion date to determine the unperformed portion of the works and impose liquidated damages for every day of    delay. If so warranted, the Management could terminate the contract then take over such or resort to any of other alternative methods of procurement provided under RA 9184, or, allow the Contractor to continue the works without prejudice to the continued imposition of liquidated damages until the works have been completed.
observations.managementAction: LCE will direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to conduct judicious, rigorous and prompt monitoring and validation of ongoing and completed IPs.  If projects were not done on time, management will make necessary action to communicate with the contractor and if it is deemed unjustified, management will impose liquidated damages or may terminate the contract.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management direct the BAC and TWG to:

a. 	Strictly scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and equipment on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual project, only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period. The winning contractor is required to supplement its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the projects awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the provisions of Sections 34.1 and 34.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

b. 	Efficiently evaluate during the bid evaluation and post qualification the accuracy of the List of All Ongoing Government and Private Contracts and the NFCC of the bidders who were awarded several projects if they accurately reflect its financial capacity to absorb the additional works and to finance its implementation or completion. 

We have recommended further that Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to strictly and closely monitor all     projects simultaneously implemented by the contractors to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said contractors.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC through the TWG conducted post-qualification on the winning bidders. 








The BAC and its TWG ensure that bid evaluation and post qualification on the accuracy of the List of all Ongoing government and private contracts and the NFCC of the bidders who were awarded with the several projects to prevent overlapping periods of implementation to the same contractor at least within the LGU. 


The Municipal Engineer and the MPDO will closely monitor all projects simultaneously implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the LCE:
Obtain the prior authorization of the Sanggunian before acting on behalf of the Municipality especially on fiscal matters involving the acceptance and utilization of donated funds.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Recognize the unrecorded cash donations amounting to ₱211,960.00 and its subsequent utilization in the Trust Fund Book;
Ensure that the details of cash donations are disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required under Item VII (2)(c) of COA Circular No. 2014-002;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines of donations required under Section 5.1.12 of Item V and Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Item VII(2)(c) of COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
Ensure compliance with the laws on procurement embodied in RA No. 9184 and keep themselves abreast with the latest issuances of the GPPB especially on emergency procurement so as to not repeat the deficiencies noted.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the MDRMMC to judiciously plan PPAs under the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund by giving priority to COVID-19 related PPAs in consonance with the SB Resolution No. 034 and in support to the National Government’s measures and proclamations to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the disposal committee to:
Conduct inventory of unserviceable properties and create disposal and appraisal committee;
Submit the required documents prior to the disposal or divestment of assets; and
Request for COA-TAS for the inspection of the assets before demolition proper be done;
We have further recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the approved documents for demolition including the duly approved IIRUP to the Municipal Accountant to serve as a basis to drop the previous asset accounts
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Drop from the books the demolished previous asset based on the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property; and
Record the value of materials salvaged from the demolished old plaza, if any.

We have recommended that the LCE make proper representation and coordinate with the DPWH to facilitate the recognition of the turn-over LIP specifically the Multi-Purpose Building as asset of the LGU.
We have recommended further to refer to Audit Observation No. 4 for the proper procedure on disposal/divestment of assets and accordingly, derecognize from the books of accounts the carrying amount of demolished assets related to the Multi-Purpose Building.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material balances to ensure that relevant information are available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Budget Officer to confer with the Municipal Accountant during the budget preparation to ensure that appropriate and correct accounts are made relative to the nature of the expenditures as PPAs to be implemented.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the following concerned heads of offices:
Municipal Engineer to:
a. Closely coordinate with barangays and Assessor’s Office in the monitoring of construction/improvement of buildings with no permit;
b. Aside from copies of issued Building Permits and approved building plans, furnish the Assessor’s Office copies of issued Occupancy Permit as reference and support in their inspection and appraisal; and
c. Monitor the issuance of building and occupancy permits.
Municipal Assessor to:
Conduct field inspection in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and Bureau of Fire Protection; and
Comply with Section 6, Chapter IV of MRPAAO and require the Municipal Engineer copies of Certificate of Occupancy Permit issued as reference and support in the appraisal of buildings.

We have recommended that the LCE, with the assistance of the Local Finance Committee, to:
Judiciously and rigorously plan, budget, and evaluate the proposed procurement of parcels of land under the 20% DF in order to avoid the occurrence of the foregoing circumstances, such as conducting a feasibility study, legal and technical consultations to ensure the viability of the intended infrastructure projects in the future; and
Scrupulously monitor the procured parcels of land and ensure that the intended infrastructure projects fall under the allowable projects listed under existing DILG-DBM JMCs.
Moreover, we have recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor to comply and submit the lacking required documentary requirements; and
Lastly, we have recommended that the LCE strictly adhere to the stipulations promulgated in every SB Resolution, and if so warranted, obtain prior authorization of the Sanggunian before acting on behalf of the Municipality especially on fiscal matters involving the utilization of funds.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the following:
Municipal Accountant to submit the missing and required documents for further verification and evaluation of the Audit Team; and
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to:
Keenly, meticulously, and scrupulously prepare the required documents with maximum care and competence; and
Verify its records in order to correct the inconsistencies and arrive with the proper and correct information of the subject documents. Thereafter, submit the documents for verification of the Audit Team.

We have recommended that the Management through the BAC and TWG conduct strict, extensive and efficient bid-evaluation and post-qualification by scrupulously scrutinizing the documents submitted by the bidder particularly the Statement of All On-going Government and Private Contracts, List of Contractor’s Personnel of contractors and List of Contractor’s Equipment.

We have recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to conduct judicious, rigorous and prompt monitoring and validation of their ongoing and completed IPs. If upon monitoring and validation on the projected or contracted completion date the Management reasonably determined that the completion of the IP will already incur delay, require the Contractor to submit a SWA as of the projected or contracted completion date to determine the unperformed portion of the works and impose liquidated damages for every day of delay. If so warranted, the Management could terminate the contract then take over such or resort to any of other alternative methods of procurement provided under RA 9184, or, allow the Contractor to continue the works without prejudice to the continued imposition of liquidated damages until the works have been completed.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to present the correct amounts pertaining to the appropriation and obligation under the current year and continuing appropriations for Republic Act No. 7171 and Republic Act No. 8240 in the 2019 Statement of Comparison of Budgeted and Actual Amounts. 

We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain registries for the said fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of recorded parcels of land in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 39 of PD 1445 to establish ownership thereof and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality. 

We have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to exercise added endeavor to locate documents and determine the details of unidentified land accounts for proper recognition and establishment of ownership thereon.

b.	237 Parcels of land identified by the Assessor as property of the LGU were not recorded in the books.

We have recommended that the Management:

1.	Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to exert further efforts in retrieval of documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation of the unrecorded land to facilitate its recording in the books. at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction;

2.	Make the necessary adjustment by recording the land account at acquisition cost, or fair value, if acquired through non-exchange transaction, based on available supporting documents; and



3. 	Initiate the transfer of ownership of road lots to various barangays which are not held for use by the Municipal Government.


c. Disbursements for 4 parcels of land amounting to P19,326,900.00 lack the required supporting documents to establish the legality, propriety and validity of the claims.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the Internal Auditor and one who certifies the validity of transaction and completeness of supporting documents to comply with the Fundamental Principles for Disbursement of Public Funds as provided under Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445. 

It is further recommended that deficiencies in the transactions be addressed with and submitted to the Audit Team for further verification of the legality, propriety and validity of                                                                                    the transactions.

The Management will exert effort to locate documents and details of unidentified land accounts for proper recognition of ownership.






The Municipal Accountant, the GSO and the Municipal Assessor will make coordination with each other and exert further efforts in the retrieval of documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation.


Municipal Accountant recorded the parcels of lands partially based on the documents furnished or submitted by the Municipal Assessor.




The Municipal Assessor extend assistance to Barangays to expedite the transfer of road lots to respective barangays.





The Municipal Accountant strictly observed the recommendations of the audit team.





The Municipal Accountant submitted the deficiencies in the transactions to the Audit team.

The Property Officer and Municipal Accountant maintain and update the property/supply ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers, respectively, reconcile the same and make the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: For proper management and handling of the PPE, we recommended that management conduct the required physical count of the PPE as of December 31 of every year. The actual count maybe undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustment; and to pinpoint responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs. Moreover, to maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers and PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities.  We recommended further that the Inventory Committee a) prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG and b) the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements. These reports supported by subsidiary ledgers and property cards will be the bases in recording the Public Infrastructures back in the PPE accounts.

Cause the Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;

We recommended that the investment in special savings account be returned to GF current account. The projects for which the fund was intended should be immediately implemented. The needed services should not be sacrificed in favor of earning interest.

We recommended that Management should seek approval from BLGF for the continued maintenance of accounts other than those provided in Section 2 of DOF Circular 01-2017. Comply with the conditions and procedures as stated in Section 3 and 4 of the department circular.
